title: Ensure equals methods are symmetric and work for subclasses (Java)
description:
A key facet of the equals contract is that if a.equals(b) then b.equals(a), i.e. that the relationship is symmetric.
Using instanceof breaks the contract when there are subclasses, because while the child is an instanceof the parent, the parent is not an instanceof the child.
recommendation: Implement equals methods using a custom comparison logic that considers subclasses.
impact: Breaks the symmetry principle of the equals contract, leading to incorrect comparisons and potential bugs.

checks.message: Implement equals methods using a custom comparison logic that considers subclasses.
